blockingIssues.description: The Repository Protocol does not cover all current AppState data operations as required. The mission requires a protocol matching the current AppState public API exactly so AppState can later delegate all operations through it, but this interface is only a subset of that API surface.

invalidate_character('remove'/'update') only removes char_by_name entries when the map value is the same object being mutated. After rebuild(), duplicate lowercase names are last-writer-wins, so removing or renaming that winning character leaves no mapping for an older duplicate still present in the project. The cache becomes stale until a full rebuild, violating the requirement that indexes stay consistent after add/remove/update sequences.

invalidate_location('remove'/'update') has the same stale-index bug as characters: when duplicate lowercase names exist, removing or renaming the current winner deletes the key instead of falling back to the remaining duplicate. Incremental cache state diverges from rebuild() output.

There is no persistence test that reloads from disk after removing a time slot with an associated rejected deduction or rejection record. The feature contract requires cascaded deletion of Facts, Deductions, and Rejections for time-slot removal plus atomic persistence on reload, but test_timeslot_cascade_persists only covers facts. A regression leaving orphaned rejections or deductions on disk after time-slot deletion would pass this suite.

remove_character only removes the Character itself. The feature contract for json-repo-crud requires remove operations to keep CacheManager indexes consistent in real time (VAL-IDX-005/006 coverage called out in the handoff), but removing a character that has facts/deductions/rejections leaves those records behind and leaves stale triples in fact_index/pending_index/rejection_index until the later cascade feature. That means the repository state after a CRUD remove is internally inconsistent for this feature's own expectedBehavior.

remove_location has the same correctness gap as remove_character: it deletes the Location but leaves any referencing Fact/Deduction/Rejection records and dedup indexes untouched. This violates the repository's stated cache-coherence invariant for mutations and can incorrectly block future deductions with stale triples after a location delete.

remove_time_slot removes the TimeSlot entry but does not clean referencing facts/deductions/rejections or dedup indexes. Even before explicit cascade-delete support was added later, this breaks the feature's own index-consistency expectations because get_time_slot_by_id() returns None while stale triples involving the deleted time slot still remain in cache-backed dedup sets.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The transcript shows repeated failed attempts to use non-existent Create/Edit-style tools before falling back to working tools, but the available-tool guidance for this mission does not clearly reflect the actual tool surface in this environment. This caused avoidable time spent on tooling confusion.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for b7676957-3427-49b5-9d1e-6f3abfe8bc25 contains multiple failed tool calls like '## Tool: Create' and later retries before succeeding.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that `Script.metadata.characters_mentioned` stores character names (not IDs), because cascade cleanup for character removal must match by name.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: src/models/puzzle.py:56-61 defines characters_mentioned as list[str]; src/storage/json_repository.py:187-195 removes by character name; tests/test_cascade.py:161-193 verifies name-based cleanup.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The refactor-worker skill says to compare expected behavior to architecture invariants and verify cache invalidation after representative mutations, but this feature still shipped remove_* methods that left stale related records/indexes. The skill may need a stronger explicit checkpoint to inspect removal-side cache coherence, not just add/update flows.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: SKILL.md Step 5 asks workers to review 'Missing cache invalidation after mutations'; commit d252752 remove_character/remove_location/remove_time_slot only invalidate the removed entity, while tests/test_json_repository.py lacks assertions for dependent-record/index cleanup after removals.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: This mission's architecture doc already states the invariant that repository mutations must maintain cache coherence and mentions cascade delete as a key improvement, but the feature handoff claims VAL-IDX coverage without noting that remove_* semantics remained inconsistent until the follow-up cascade feature. A short note in mission guidance clarifying that pre-cascade remove operations still need coherent cache state would likely prevent this misread.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md invariant #2 says every mutation must avoid stale indexes, while commit d252752 remove_* methods leave referenced fact/deduction/rejection data untouched and tests do not check for stale dedup entries after removals.
